conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's great that you prioritized your morning routine. Can you tell me about your week so far? Any notable events or appointments?
Yeah, work has been keeping me busy. Showing some properties to clients, trying to meet targets. Honestly, it's not the most exciting stuff.
I understand. It can be challenging to balance work and personal life. How was your weekend, though? Did you have any downtime or relaxation?
Well, actually, my partner had a birthday party on Saturday, so that took up most of the day. But we did grab brunch afterwards. The food was okay, not great.
That sounds like a nice celebration. How do you find your work-life balance in general? Do you have any hobbies or interests outside of real estate?
To be honest, I don't really have much time for hobbies anymore. Sometimes I'll browse some online marketplaces or browse through home decor websites. It's not super fulfilling, though.
That makes sense. It can be hard to prioritize self-care when you're busy with work. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite recently?
Actually, yeah, I have been eating more frequently, but it's like my stomach is always growling or something. Maybe it's because I'm stressed about meeting those targets.
Stress can definitely impact our physical symptoms. Can you tell me more about what's causing that stress for you? Is there anything specific on your plate?
It's just the usual pressure to perform, I guess. The agents at my office are all competitive, and it feels like if I don't meet my numbers, I'll be seen as less capable.
That can be a tough environment to work in. Have you talked to anyone about this stress or sought any support?
Nah, not really. I just try to push through and focus on the task at hand. My partner's been trying to get me to open up more, but it's hard for me.
It sounds like there might be some underlying emotions you're not addressing. Would you say that's accurate?
Honestly? Yeah, probably. I just don't know how to deal with them sometimes. It feels silly to cry about this stuff.
Not at all! Sometimes it can help to acknowledge and validate those feelings. Let's explore that further if you're willing.
Alright... I mean, yeah, sure. Go ahead.
So what do you think is the most pressing issue right now? Would you say it's the stress from work or something else entirely?
Um, probably just work-related stuff. It's like my whole identity is wrapped up in being a successful real estate agent.
I understand how that can be all-consuming. What are some things you're looking forward to or hoping to achieve in the near future?
Hmm... maybe finding a way to balance my work and personal life better? And not feeling so burnt out all the time.
Those are great goals! Do you have any ideas on how you might start working towards those?
Honestly, I'm not really sure. That's why I'm here, right?
Exactly! And that's a great step towards making progress. Let's work together to come up with some concrete steps for you.
Sounds good to me. Thanks for listening and all.
Of course! It was my pleasure. Is there anything else on your mind that we haven't covered yet?
Not really... unless you want to know more about my favorite TV show. I'm obsessed with it!
(laughs) That's perfectly fine! We can definitely explore that if you'd like.
Yeah, okay. It's just something I do to unwind after work. The characters are so relatable and engaging.
I love that! Sometimes having a hobby or interest outside of work can really help with stress management. Do you find yourself thinking about it during the day?
Actually, yeah, sometimes I'll catch myself wondering what's going on in the latest season or who might get killed off next episode... (laughs)
(laughs) Ahah! Well, it sounds like your favorite TV show is definitely a coping mechanism for you. Have you noticed any other ways that you use to manage stress?
Honestly? Not really... I mean, like I said, I've been eating more frequently, but that's about it.
Okay, let's explore this further. What do you think would happen if you did find a way to balance your work and personal life better?
Maybe I wouldn't be so burnt out all the time? Or maybe I could actually enjoy my free time again instead of feeling guilty for not working more.
That sounds like a great place to start! It might take some experimentation, but let's try to identify some concrete steps you can take towards achieving that goal. What do you say?
Yeah, sure thing. Thanks for listening and helping me figure this out.
You're welcome! I'm here to support you. How do you feel about meeting again in a week or two to check in on your progress?
Sounds good to me. That would be helpful, yeah.
Great! I'll send you a reminder closer to the time. Is there anything else you'd like to add before we wrap up today's session?
Not really... just that it's been kind of nice talking about this stuff and having someone to share my concerns with.

That means a lot to me, thank you! It was a pleasure discussing your concerns with you. Remember, everything we talked about is completely confidential and our goal is to work together to make positive changes in your life.
